titleStmt.title: SII 10.33: original edition by J. Ramayya Pantulu
No. 33. (A.R. No. 459 of 1915.) ON THE KIRĀTĀRJUNA PILLAR ON THE TOP OF THE INDRAKĪLA HILL, BEZWADA, KRISHNA DISTRICT. Undated.

summary.p: States that the inscribed slab was planted at the spot where Arjuna made tapas on the Indrakīla hill for obtaining the Pāśupata weapon from god Śiva, by Pechchavāḍa Trikōṭibōyi, son of Kaliyamabōyi who claims to be a descendant of the Yaksha who guided Arjuna to that spot. (Published in the Journal of the Āndhra Sāhitya Parishat, Vol. 7, pp. 227—231 and in A.S.R. 1915—16, pp. 98 f.)
As read from the bottom upwards.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
A¨rjjunaḥ phalguṇaḥ pā¨rtthaḥ kirīṭī śvētavāha
naḥ vībhatsu [¨r]vvijayaḥ kriṣṇaḥ savyasāchī dhanaṁ
jayaḥ svasti śrūyatē khalu dvaitavanē pāṇḍavā
ndṛṣṭvā tatpakṣapātēna vyagrō A¨rjjuna Indrakīlaṁ gatvā
viṣṇusāhāyyatō tapasā mahendra mārādhya tadupa
dēśāt maheśvaramārādhayataḥ pāśupātāstrā
¨rtthaṁ cātigahana mindrakīlaṁ Ayaṁ yakṣaḥ prāpa
yiṣyati tvāmityādiṣṭavaniti sa ca yakṣaḥ śāpā
starātkalau peccavāḍakaliyamabōyi putraḥ
trikōṭibōyināmā dhā¨rmmikaḥ prādurabhū
dyaḥ ka¨rṇna Iva balaparākramōpētō vadā
nya śca . mātali riva sa¨rvvakā¨ryyakuśalaḥsvā
mibhaktaśca . hanūmā niva viditajanmānta
rāvatārō svakulōttamacaritaśca . saḥ pū

¨rvva majjunamaitryā jjanmāntaravēdī tatpā
śupatāstrāvāptikalā nnidhāna mindrakīlē
svayaśōnidhi miva svakulābhivṛddhayē
stambha māsthāpayat . Indrakīlōgiri
¨ryyāvat dyāvaccē ya mmahānadī tri
kōṭibōyu dha¨rmmōyaṁ sthēyā ntāva dbhavē di
ha . vijayācā¨ryyasya likhitaṁ .
Note.—The characters are of the Chāḷukyan type.
Digital edition of SII 10.33 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
